title: Deduplication at Scale
concept: Deduplication algorithms (like MinHash or LSH) find and remove duplicate documents across petabytes of text, reducing memorization and training costs.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of 'deduplication' in machine learning?

options.text: Finding and removing duplicate copies of text
Making the text twice as long
Translating text into five different languages

exercises.explain: Deduplication means finding and removing identical or near-identical copies of data.

cards.title: Too Much Copy-Pasting!
cards.body: Did you know that AI models can accidentally memorize the exact same webpage thousands of times? This wastes computer memory and makes the AI repeat itself. Let's learn how we clean up petabytes of text to keep AI smart and efficient!

cards.title: What is Deduplication?
cards.body: Deduplication at scale uses smart math algorithms (like MinHash or Locality-Sensitive Hashing) to compress documents into tiny signatures. By comparing these signatures, we can quickly spot and delete duplicate texts across massive datasets.

cards.title: The Fingerprint Scanner
cards.body: Imagine trying to clean out millions of duplicate printed sheets from a giant library. Instead of reading every single word on every page to compare them, you just look at a tiny, unique 'fingerprint' stamped on each page. If two pages have the exact same fingerprint, they are almost certainly duplicates!

cards.title: Deduplication in Action
cards.body: If a web dataset contains 10,000 copies of the same terms-of-service agreement, deduplication spots the identical signatures and keeps only one. This stops the AI from repeating the agreement word-for-word during chats.

cards.title: The Slow Comparison Myth
cards.body: Many think deduplication requires looking at every single word in every document. In reality, algorithms like LSH look at tiny, clever mathematical summaries, finding duplicates in minutes instead of years.

cards.title: Before vs. After Deduplication
cards.body: See how deduplication transforms messy, redundant data into a clean, unique dataset for training.

left.text: Millions of identical news articles, terms of service, and copy-pasted blog posts.

right.text: One clean copy of each unique text, reducing dataset size and saving training energy.

exercises.prompt: What is a negative side effect of training an AI on data that has NOT been deduplicated?

options.text: The AI wastes brainpower memorizing the exact same text over and over.
The AI completely forgets how to read English.
The AI becomes too fast at answering questions.
The AI runs out of colors to display on the screen.

exercises.explain: When an AI is trained on duplicate data, it memorizes and overfits to those repetitive phrases, wasting training capacity and outputting repetitive answers.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Removing duplicate text documents increases the total time and cost to train an AI model.
exercises.explain: Deduplication actually reduces training costs because the model has less repetitive data to process, saving time and energy.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete how deduplication algorithms compare files efficiently.
exercises.sentence: Deduplication algorithms convert whole documents into small, unique mathematical _______ to compare them quickly.
exercises.explain: By converting large documents into small mathematical signatures, we can quickly find duplicate files without reading all their text.

exercises.prompt: Which AI describes a realistic, scalable deduplication strategy for petabytes of data?

options.text: To clean up our 10-petabyte dataset, I will write a script that opens every single document and compares every letter one-by-one with every other document.
To clean our dataset, I will use MinHash to generate a small signature for each document, then group similar signatures together to delete duplicates.
I will randomly delete 50% of all documents in the dataset, which guarantees we remove half of the duplicate pages without looking at them.

exercises.explain: AI B uses the word 'signature' and 'hash values' which correctly describes how modern scale deduplication algorithms (like MinHash/LSH) work. AI A is trying to manually read everything, which is too slow.

exercises.prompt: You have petabytes of text data and need to remove duplicates before training your model. What is the most scalable way to do this?

options.text: Reading and comparing every single word of every document side-by-side
Comparing compact digital fingerprints (hashes) of the documents instead of the full text
Manually hiring humans to read through petabytes of data
Deleting random files until the dataset size shrinks by half

exercises.explain: Deduplication tools use smart hashing (like MinHash) to compare compact 'fingerprints' rather than reading every single word of every document, allowing us to clean up massive datasets quickly.
